t1: Mini-STRC doesn't just survive truncation — it folds significantly better without the N-terminal. The disordered half was actively destabilizing the protein. Li et al. (2026) showed that AF3 builds structural constraints hierarchically: intra-chain geometry is established first, then inter-chain contacts are inferred from it. A large disordered N-terminus disrupts this process — removing it lets AF3 establish consistent geometric constraints across the entire chain, explaining the pTM jump from 0.63 to 0.81.
t2: 4. AlphaFold 3 confirms: removing the N-terminal improves the fold
